title: syzygium iteophyllum
intro: Syzygium iteophyllum, also known as the Philippine water apple or water berry, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various folk medicine practices due to its medicinal properties, including antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Syzygium iteophyllum include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. Syzygium iteophyllum is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
